trends_result.evidence: Interest: 41/100 now vs 58/100 six weeks ago · Peak: 100/100 · geo: IN · 90 days

social_result.evidence: Web-indexed social mentions · 5 results · Note: search results about social content, not direct Instagram data

news_result.evidence: 2 articles in past 2 months · 2 fashion-relevant signals
top_headlines: This Is Where The Lucknow Locals Are Buying Their Chikankari From
Fit check: Chennai college students stitch style between freedom and dress codes

bet_override: Override: demand signals are weak despite active editorial/social buzz. Social buzz may not convert to value-fashion sales. Watch 4 weeks before any commitment.

india_fit.price_band_reason: Chikankari kurtis at ₹399–₹1,499 align with value-fashion positioning; embroidery-heavy variants command premiums within this range.
india_fit.climate_fit_reason: Chikankari's lightweight cotton construction suits festive/winter layering and is culturally timed to Diwali–New Year gifting cycles in North India.
india_fit.occasion_fit: festive, ethnic, wedding, family gatherings, semi-formal
india_fit.cultural_fit_reason: Chikankari is a heritage craft with strong regional (Lucknow) and pan-Indian prestige; aligns with festive modesty norms and aspirational value-fashion positioning.
india_fit.value_fashion_fit_reason: Meesho shows moderate catalog presence (10 listings), but discount signals (2) suggest margin pressure; Vishal/FabIndia value-segment customers buy chikankari, but sustainability of markup is unclear at ₹399–₹799 entry points.

claude_synthesis.convergence_summary: Demand is cooling (Google Trends down 29% in 6 weeks) while editorial buzz remains steady; signals suggest chikankari has moved from trend spike into established seasonal category with stable but not growing interest.
claude_synthesis.signal_agreement: Partial disagreement: demand curves downward, but news coverage and social mentions remain active—suggesting buzz may be trailing falling demand rather than predicting future demand.
claude_synthesis.disagreement_note: Search Myntra/Meesho sales rank and return-on-ad-spend (ROAS) for chikankari vs. plain cotton kurtis in same price band over past 4 weeks to confirm whether price-conscious buyers are shifting away from embroidered styles.
claude_synthesis.bet_reasoning: Google Trends' 29-point drop in 6 weeks is a concrete demand signal; however, it is a 90-day view and peaked earlier in the season, suggesting this is a predictable festive-cycle dip before Oct–Jan surge. Marketplace catalog presence (moderate) and news coverage (active, location-specific) suggest structural demand exists, but entry inventory should be conservative and skew toward ₹699–₹999 sweet-spot to avoid clearance markdown risk at lower price points.
claude_synthesis.skepticism_flag: Google Trends shows decay, but search interest often contracts *before* festive shopping season accelerates (Aug–Sep trough is normal for Oct–Jan inventory); this signal may be a false negative if the buyer's Tier 2–3 customer base shops late (Sept–Oct), not now.
